repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 records ThmSmith/PartialID as empty seed, and prior pid_long_term_surrogate angle0 was generic drift-LP tightening rather than finite noisy-proxy sign ambiguity

published_prior_art.cite: ChenGengJia2007/YinLiuGengLuo2020: surrogate-paradox sign reversal and observationally equivalent paradox laws; proposal adds randomized finite noisy proxy panel plus bounded drift

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue ThmSmith proposal states the K=2 noisy-proxy joint bridge-measurement-drift compatibility boundary

published_prior_art.cite: closest are DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 and FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2021, which give generic discrete partial-ID/measurement-error programs, not this closed-form surrogate boundary

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The body invokes Manski/Balke--Pearl style sharpness and a Manski no-information bound, but §13 has no Manski or Balke--Pearl bibliography entries.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The literature comparison misses Finkelstein, Adams, Saria, and Shpitser (2021, UAI/PMLR), which is closer prior art for partial identification with discrete measurement error and LP bounds.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The why-new block claims sign ambiguity despite a uniformly positive fixed surrogate-index margin, but the formal statement only assumes a strictly positive margin and could use an arbitrarily small one.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Manski, Balke--Pearl, and Finkelstein--Adams--Saria--Shpitser to related work and explain precisely why the proposed K=2 formula is not just their generic LP/polynomial-program encoding.
Tighten Conjecture 1 by either stating an explicit positive margin lower bound or removing the fixed-margin language from the novelty claim.
Split Conjecture 2 into a definitional joint-image lemma and a genuinely nontrivial closed-form K=2 boundary theorem/conjecture with explicit endpoint formulas or quantifier-eliminated inequalities.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal around the explicit K=2 closed-form boundary and patch the missing measurement-error partial-ID prior art before returning to Stage 0.
